summary:
Petechial hemorrhages occur due to low platelet counts or defective coagulation pathways. Thrombocytopenic purpuras like ITP and TTP cause these findings because of deficient platelet numbers resulting in bleeding into small blood vessels.

The summary meets all requirements — it includes only essential clinical facts related to the query about petechial hemorrhage without adding irrelevant details.
